jd.location: Nishi-Shinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo; 3 days in office and 2 days remote work available
responsibilities: and operation of AI agent quality-evaluation infrastructure
Research and develop evaluation metrics including LLM-as-Judge calibration
and maintain evaluation benchmarks and evaluation sets using synthetic data and real logs
Design and implement scalable automated evaluation pipelines
Integrate evaluation pipelines into production CI/CD and build scalable quality gates
Design agent evaluation harnesses supporting multi-turn interactions
Ensure reproducibility and reliability of evaluation pipelines
Research and implement automated red teaming and adversarial testing
Build safety and policy compliance verification frameworks
Research and implement hallucination detection and calibration methods
Design and execute prompt and tool regression tests
Design and analyze statistical experiments including A/B tests and significance testing
Visualize quality trends and automate regression detection
Create quality reports and improvement proposals
Feed evaluation signals back to research and development teams
Ensure production product quality for products used by approximately 200 companies
requirements: 3+ years of practical experience as an ML Engineer
Research or implementation experience in ML model evaluation or LLM evaluation
Deep knowledge of LLM and generative AI evaluation methods including benchmark design
Practical knowledge of statistics and experimental design including hypothesis testing
Experience building ML or evaluation pipelines in Python
Practical experience with machine learning frameworks such as PyTorch
Experience designing and implementing evaluation metrics beyond precision/recall
At least one of the following language capabilities: fluent Japanese able to discuss product development without friction
preferredQualifications: Publication experience at top ML/NLP conferences such as NeurIPS
Research or implementation experience with reward modeling or preference learning such as RLHF or DPO
Experience with LLM-as-Judge calibration and rubric design
Knowledge or experience in AI safety
Experience with benchmark design and validity verification such as IRT or construct validity
Large-scale data processing experience such as Spark or BigQuery
Experience integrating ML or evaluation pipelines into CI/CD
jd.employerSignal: JAPAN AI, Inc. was established in April 2023 as a group company of Geniee, Inc. on the TSE Growth Market. The company is rapidly expanding its enterprise AI agent suite including JAPAN AI AGENT, CHAT, and SPEECH. The role sits in a new specialized organization focused on scientifically evaluating AI output quality, safety, and reliability for products used by approximately 200 companies. The development organization has approximately 120 members, and the broader startup has grown to 200+ people and 9 products in 3 years.
risks: Role requires specialized ML/LLM evaluation research or implementation experience
Requires strong statistics and experimental design capability
Requires Python ML/evaluation pipeline experience and practical ML framework experience
Language requirement requires either fluent Japanese for product development discussions or business-level English
Fixed overtime of 45 hours is included in monthly compensation
Greenfield role may require building evaluation methodology and production infrastructure from scratch
High accountability because evaluation infrastructure is described as the last line of defense for release quality
jd.fitNotes: Strong fit for candidates with ML, data science, research engineering, or AI evaluation backgrounds who can combine statistical experimental design, LLM evaluation methodology, automated pipelines, and AI safety practices. The role is less suitable for candidates whose experience is primarily manual testing or business analysis without ML evaluation, Python pipeline, statistics, or LLM evaluation depth.

positioning.primaryHiringProblem: JAPAN AI needs a technical evaluation scientist who can build rigorous, scalable, statistically valid LLM and agent quality infrastructure for enterprise AI products.
positioning.managerHireReason: The candidate can show practical AI quality operations, chatbot evaluation, A/B attribution, GPT scoring workflows, human-review loops, governance routing, and production AI chatbot operations, but does not fully meet the ML research, Python pipeline, statistics, or ML framework depth implied by the role.
positioning.safestPositioning: Enterprise AI quality operations specialist with chatbot evaluation, GPT scoring workflow, A/B testing design, human-review QA loop, and governance automation experience.
positioning.headlineRecommendation: AI Quality Operations / Conversational AI Evaluation Specialist with enterprise chatbot evaluation, GPT scoring, A/B testing, and governance automation experience.
hiddenSkillsToSurface: Python/SQL-style pipeline inspection and validation as maintenance, not from-scratch engineering
AI coding tool assisted debugging as interview context only
evidenceToSuppress: evi-ai-coding-tool-assisted-debugging: interview only, not strong visible CV proof
evi-research-lab-management: too weak for this JD's scientist requirement
Raw TOMO, FIN, Trender Buddy, and internal benchmark score names should be replaced with external-friendly wording
claimsToAvoid: AI Evaluation Scientist with production ML ownership
PyTorch, JAX, or TensorFlow practical ownership

managerIntent.actualJobToBeDone: Build a scientific and production-ready evaluation function that prevents low-quality or unsafe AI-agent behavior from reaching enterprise customers.
hiringManagerPainPoints: AI products are scaling quickly and need reliable quality gates
Existing product teams likely need more scientific evaluation methods than ad hoc testing
AI agents introduce multi-turn, tool-use, long-context, and safety risks
Enterprise customers require quality, safety, reproducibility, and accountability
The organization needs evaluation signals that R&D teams can act on
successSignals: Evaluation pipelines run reliably and reproducibly
Benchmarks and rubrics catch regressions before release
Quality reports clearly drive product improvements
Metrics are statistically defensible and not just subjective QA scores
Safety, hallucination, and policy compliance checks reduce release risk
dealBreakers: No real ML or LLM evaluation implementation experience
Cannot code or maintain Python evaluation pipelines
No practical statistics or experimental design ability
No ML framework exposure where required
Only manual QA or business analysis experience
Cannot communicate in business-level English or fluent Japanese

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: 3+ years practical experience as ML Engineer, Data Scientist, Research Engineer, or ML/AI evaluation-related role
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Recruiter will look for formal ML/data science/evaluation job titles or deep hands-on ML evaluation ownership.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: AI quality evaluation operations for enterprise chatbot and internal AI assistant workflows.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: Candidate titles and evidence do not clearly show ML Engineer, Data Scientist, Research Engineer, or formal ML evaluation scientist experience.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Research or implementation experience in ML model evaluation or LLM evaluation
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Hands-on LLM evaluation design, metrics, datasets, and reproducible evaluation methods.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Built practical GPT/chatbot evaluation workflows combining scoring, human review, and metric tracking.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: Evidence supports operational evaluation but not formal ML model evaluation research.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Deep knowledge of benchmark design, LLM-as-Judge, quantitative quality measurement, and hallucination detection
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Can discuss rigorous benchmarks, judge calibration, hallucination methods, and quantitative validity.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Structured benchmark-style chatbot evaluation and GPT-based scoring workflow.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: No direct evidence of LLM-as-Judge calibration, hallucination detection methodology, construct validity, or contamination detection.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Statistics and experimental design including hypothesis testing, A/B testing, confidence intervals, and effect sizes
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Can design and interpret statistical experiments, not just route traffic or track metrics.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Designed live A/B routing and reporting logic for chatbot comparison.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: Evidence does not prove hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, effect sizes, or statistical significance analysis.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Experience building ML or evaluation pipelines in Python
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Hands-on Python engineering for evaluation pipeline design, execution, validation, and maintenance.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Built GPT-based evaluation workflow and inspected existing Python/SQL-style automation logic for validation and troubleshooting.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: Do not claim from-scratch Python ML pipeline engineering unless source can prove code ownership.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Practical experience with PyTorch, JAX, or TensorFlow
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Hands-on ML framework usage for model/evaluation implementation.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: No supported evidence for PyTorch, JAX, or TensorFlow.

jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Can design task success, factuality, win rate, rubric, harm, calibration, or other domain-specific metrics.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Defined chatbot quality metrics, GPT scoring categories, human review loop, and A/B reporting measures.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: No proof of proper scoring rules, win rate modeling, factuality calibration, or harm metrics.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: AI safety, Responsible AI, red teaming, adversarial testing, and policy compliance verification
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Can build safety and compliance verification frameworks for AI systems.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Translated AI/privacy governance requirements into executable risk scoring, routing, and reviewer controls.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: Governance evidence is strong, but not automated red teaming or adversarial testing.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Multi-turn, tool-use, long-context, or AI-agent evaluation harnesses
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Can evaluate complex AI-agent behavior, tool calls, and long-context workflows.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: AI assistant and chatbot quality evaluation workflow experience.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: No direct evidence of multi-agent, long-context, or tool-use evaluation harness implementation.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: CI/CD quality gates and scalable automated evaluation infrastructure
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Evaluation checks integrated into release pipelines and production engineering workflows.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Automated quality workflow and production data-pipeline validation support.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: No direct evidence of CI/CD quality gate ownership or scalable ML evaluation infrastructure.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Business-level English or fluent Japanese for product development discussion
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Can collaborate technically in English or Japanese without friction.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Cross-region Global/Japan AI chatbot operations and Philippines Workday KT facilitation.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: No formal language proficiency evidence provided; do not claim fluent Japanese.

internalTerminology.reason: The concept is useful but should be explained as a benchmark-style evaluation set.

internalTerminology.reason: External readers need the compliance function more than the internal app name.

internalTerminology.externalFriendlyWording: learning data synchronization job into analytics data layer
internalTerminology.reason: Internal job name is too specific and not suitable for CV wording.

internalTerminology.externalFriendlyWording: automated recruiting alert for long-open requisitions with no candidate pipeline
internalTerminology.reason: Internal workflow name should be converted into business outcome.

remainingGaps.gap: No supported PyTorch, JAX, or TensorFlow experience
remainingGaps.mitigation: Do not list these tools. If applying, complete and document a small LLM evaluation notebook using one framework or state no direct framework experience in interview.

remainingGaps.gap: Limited proof of advanced statistics: hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, effect sizes, significance testing
remainingGaps.mitigation: CV should only claim A/B routing and metric definition. Prepare an interview explanation of what was measured and avoid claiming statistical inference unless documented.

remainingGaps.gap: No direct reward modeling, RLHF, DPO, proper scoring rules, IRT, construct validity, or contamination detection evidence
remainingGaps.mitigation: Do not include these as skills. Treat them as learning gaps and ask whether the role can accept AI quality operations background.

remainingGaps.gap: Python evaluation pipeline ownership is only partially supported
remainingGaps.mitigation: Frame Python as existing pipeline inspection, validation, and troubleshooting unless source confirms from-scratch pipeline code ownership.

remainingGaps.gap: No CI/CD evaluation quality gate ownership
remainingGaps.mitigation: Use production monitoring and automated GPT evaluation workflow language, not CI/CD gate ownership.

remainingGaps.gap: Language requirement is not fully evidenced
remainingGaps.mitigation: State only verified business communication languages and avoid claiming fluent Japanese.

analysis.summaryAngle: Stretch application. The CV should not try to look like a pure AI Evaluation Scientist. The strongest safe angle is enterprise AI quality operations: chatbot evaluation framework, GPT scoring workflow, A/B attribution metrics, production AI chatbot operations, and AI governance controls. Major ML/statistics/framework gaps must remain visible internally so the CV generator does not overclaim.

Master of Biomedical Sciences plus AI/Big Data training and scientific research operations background.
Cross-region Global/Japan AI chatbot operations and Philippines Workday KT facilitation.

recommendedPositioning.headline: AI Quality Operations / Conversational AI Evaluation Specialist with enterprise chatbot evaluation, GPT scoring, A/B testing, and governance automation experience.
recommendedPositioning.summaryAngle: Stretch application. The CV should not try to look like a pure AI Evaluation Scientist. The strongest safe angle is enterprise AI quality operations: chatbot evaluation framework, GPT scoring workflow, A/B attribution metrics, production AI chatbot operations, and AI governance controls. Major ML/statistics/framework gaps must remain visible internally so the CV generator does not overclaim.
wordingGuidance: Enterprise AI quality operations specialist with chatbot evaluation, GPT scoring workflow, A/B testing design, human-review QA loop, and governance automation experience.
Use adjacent-fit wording and explain weak evidence as risk.
Strengthen wording only when supported by selected evidence.
Preserve unsupported requirements as gaps or risks, not visible strengths.

recommendedPositioning.headline: AI Quality Operations / Conversational AI Evaluation Specialist with enterprise chatbot evaluation, GPT scoring, A/B testing, and governance automation experience.
recommendedPositioning.summaryAngle: Stretch application. The CV should not try to look like a pure AI Evaluation Scientist. The strongest safe angle is enterprise AI quality operations: chatbot evaluation framework, GPT scoring workflow, A/B attribution metrics, production AI chatbot operations, and AI governance controls. Major ML/statistics/framework gaps must remain visible internally so the CV generator does not overclaim.
wordingGuidance: Enterprise AI quality operations specialist with chatbot evaluation, GPT scoring workflow, A/B testing design, human-review QA loop, and governance automation experience.
Use adjacent-fit wording and explain weak evidence as risk.
Strengthen wording only when supported by selected evidence.
Preserve unsupported requirements as gaps or risks, not visible strengths.

requiredCriteria.label: Role identity
requiredCriteria.description: States a credible role identity for this JD without pretending unsupported direct-fit experience.

requiredCriteria.label: Relevant capability
requiredCriteria.description: Surfaces the strongest supported capabilities that overlap with the JD.

requiredCriteria.label: Business value
requiredCriteria.description: Explains the business or stakeholder value of the candidate's supported work.

requiredCriteria.label: Evidence grounding
requiredCriteria.description: Avoids unsupported ownership, metrics, seniority, sales, architecture, or cloud-delivery claims.

requiredCriteria.label: Customer context
requiredCriteria.description: Connects the Summary to customer, stakeholder, adoption, enablement, or operating context when supported.

requiredCriteria.label: Career positioning
requiredCriteria.description: Separates supported transferable fit from unsupported core requirements.

requiredCriteria.label: Clarity
requiredCriteria.description: Uses concise external recruiter language without generic filler or tool stuffing.

careerProfile.claimBoundaries: Use parsed snapshots as source of truth. Do not invent exact revenue, savings, team size, active user count, formal certifications, formal language level, or external product ownership beyond the source. Prefer built, supported, coordinated, participated, evaluated, maintained, took over, served as BA/interface, or led design sessions according to source-specific wording. Treat internal IDs, endpoint URLs, PO amounts, app/report/environment IDs, named internal stakeholders, workspace IDs, account names, and vendor contacts as internal proof, not external CV wording. Do not claim formal Japanese proficiency from bilingual chatbot content. Do not claim quantified business impact beyond metrics explicitly stated. Preserve title and period conflicts until verified.

selectedSkills.skill: GPT evaluation and human-review QA loop
selectedSkills.cvWording: Build closed-loop AI quality workflows combining automated GPT scoring, human review, and prompt iteration.
selectedSkills.evidenceSummary: Built GPT auto-evaluation pipeline and Power App review form for Trender Buddy, creating a closed QA loop from GPT review to human review to prompt revision.

selectedSkills.skill: Live chatbot A/B test routing design
selectedSkills.cvWording: Design live A/B routing, attribution, and reporting logic for AI platform evaluation.
selectedSkills.evidenceSummary: Designed cookie-based routing where odd suffix showed FIN and even suffix showed TOMO, specified UTM attribution and D365 Omnichannel routing, and defined five reporting metrics for live chatbot comparison.

selectedSkills.cvWording: Own production operations for customer-service AI chatbot platforms across global support markets.
selectedSkills.evidenceSummary: Owned customer-service AI chatbot operations for Global and Japan consumer support markets, including workspace administration, license tracking, CSM coordination, and 193 change requests or bug fixes from 2024/12 to 2025/12.

selectedSkills.cvWording: Evaluate GenAI tools for NL-to-SQL feasibility, business usability, infrastructure dependency, and production-risk boundaries.

selectedSkills.cvWording: Coordinate learning data pipelines across Docebo, Jenkins, Python, Azure Blob Storage, ADX, and Workday reference data.

selectedStories.title: Replaced subjective chatbot platform comparison with data-driven evaluation
selectedStories.action: Combined Golden Question Test datasets, real transcript GPT scoring, human review validation, and live A/B test planning; defined scoring standards and five reporting metric areas.
selectedStories.result: Produced quantitative comparison evidence including Global and Japan benchmark results, giving stakeholders a structured basis for platform selection.
cvBullets: Created a structured chatbot platform evaluation framework combining benchmark questions, transcript scoring, human review, and live A/B metrics to support data-driven platform selection.

selectedStories.title: Built scheduled GPT evaluation for internal employee AI assistant quality
selectedStories.action: Built a daily GPT-based evaluation pipeline using Azure AI Studio and FastAPI, maintained prompt versions across eight categories, and defined High/Medium/Low scoring rules for answer quality.
selectedStories.result: Replaced manual conversation review with daily scheduled GPT scoring at 8:30 AM and created a repeatable quality signal for AI assistant improvement.
cvBullets: Built an automated GPT evaluation pipeline for an internal employee AI assistant, replacing manual conversation review with scheduled scoring and versioned prompt quality rules.

selectedStories.title: Defined attribution logic for live dual-chatbot A/B reporting
selectedStories.action: Defined odd/even cookie routing, UTM attribution, D365 Omnichannel queue mapping, TOMO/FIN identifier logic using caid and case flags, and Power BI metrics for handover, abandon, feedback, CSAT, and DSAT.
selectedStories.result: Enabled engineering reporting logic that separated TOMO and FIN performance during live testing and supported weekly metric tracking.
cvBullets: Defined live A/B attribution logic for chatbot comparison, including cookie routing, UTM tracking, Omnichannel queue mapping, platform identifiers, and Power BI metric definitions.

selectedStories.title: Clarified NL-to-SQL tool feasibility and production risk
selectedStories.action: Performed a POC and documented structured pros and cons, including quick demo value, prompt adjustability, infrastructure dependency, SQL generation error risk, Non-RD operability, and production execution concerns.
selectedStories.result: Clarified that Dify was useful for quick POCs but risky for direct production database execution without controls.
cvBullets: Evaluated a self-hosted GenAI NL-to-SQL platform, documenting feasibility, infrastructure dependency, user operability, and production database risk boundaries.

selectedStories.title: Automated customer-service chatbot knowledge-base synchronization
selectedStories.action: Built a three-stage Power Automate synchronization flow using Eureka API, Excel tracking, and chatbot workspace update logic, with separate flows for retrieving Eureka data, updating chatbot content, and mapping chatbot IDs.
selectedStories.result: Daily KB synchronization replaced manual article upload risk and supported ongoing chatbot content operations across Global and Japan markets.
cvBullets: Built daily chatbot knowledge-base synchronization using Power Automate and Eureka API, reducing manual article upload risk and keeping chatbot content aligned with source KB updates.

selectedStories.title: Stabilized post-launch customer-service AI chatbot operations
selectedStories.action: Managed workspace operations, tracked change requests and operation logs, coordinated vendor/business escalation, validated fixes in staging before production, and handled issues including telephone-number exposure, JP chat reporting, and JP CSAT decline.
selectedStories.result: Supported sustained chatbot operations through 193 post-launch change request and bug-fix versions and 88 operation log versions while keeping business and vendor issue handling traceable.
cvBullets: Owned configuration and operations for a customer-service AI chatbot across Global and Japan consumer support markets, covering workspace administration, routing, content guidance, KB automation, production issue tracking, and vendor coordination.
